utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next patient is randy gutierrez . date of birth , 8/10/2020 . please use review of symptoms . all text to write . physical exam , auto text uri .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , how are you doing today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we're okay , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's good to hear . so , how many days has randy been feeling sick ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i would say it started around supper time last night .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: last night , okay , and what's been going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , he started to get a runny nose .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and is he stuffy too ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and have you noticed , is his mucous clear right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it is , it is right now , yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , does he also have a cough ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , but it's not barky .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and does he act like he has a sore throat or is he pulling on his ears ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i know he's been pulling on his ears , yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you know , he also , he's also been going crazy rubbing at his nose too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , any fever you've noticed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i checked his rectal temperature and it was 100.3 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , a little bit of a low-grade fever there , definitely . and , how was his appetite ? did he eat last night ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , he did . yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about this morning ? did he eat his breakfast ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , this morning he ate about seven ounces from the bottle .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then he got another bottle , and he barely ate that . um , i offered him cereal and he would n't eat that either .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , he varies i guess .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how is his energy ? does he seem like he wants to take more naps , or does he have pretty good energy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mmm , it seems like he's irritable .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: irritable because he is n't feeling good ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . he is energetic , but it seems like he ca n't go to sleep , like , he's fighting it really hard .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and just for the chart , is there anyone , anybody else exposed to him who has been sick ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , his older sister's been sick .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we also had a play date with my nephew , who i learned later had a sinus infection .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and what medication have you tried for him ? anything at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i've been doing the saline mist in his nose .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good , good . that's a good choice .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did give him tylenol really early this morning because he was warm when i took the sleeper off of him , and we had the ac on in the house , but his whole body was sweating .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see . and , any other medications ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we did put some baby vick's on his feet last night to try and help him breathe . and , i also used the humidifier .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that's good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , there was another thing i wanted to bring up since we're here . it appears that his cradle cap has gotten a little worse . what are you using on it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been using the cradle cap brush , and then i use regular aveno shampoo .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is it a dandruff shampoo ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do n't think so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , thank you . well , let's complete his exam and then we'll talk about the next steps .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: can you take a big breath randy .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: where's mama ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do n't forget to breathe little one . okay . let's try the front too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it's okay buddy .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: excellent . he's doing excellent . next let's- let's check out those ears .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's try to make sure you do n't tip all the way over .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: dino , you're doing so good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . ears look okay . we're going to slide you forward so you do n't bump your head when i lay you down .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , you're- hey you're okay . you're okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , you can go ahead and sit him back up if you like .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , he's just kind of getting started with this , and i think we're seeing something viral right now . often sinus infections will start out as a virus and then will become bacterial infections if left alone and does n't go away . but , i do n't think he needs any antibiotics , at least not at this point in time . um , keep up with the fluids , rest , and i would watch him very carefully for a barking cough . if he does get a barky cough , then that tends to be a little bit more significant and a little more severe . so , if he develops a barky cough , i want you to give him a half a teaspoon of his sister's medicine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you know , i'm almost tempted to give you some of the medicine because they're probably sharing the same virus .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: actually , i will . i'll go ahead and just give you some as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but , if he does n't become barky , you do n't have to use it . it only works for the barky cough .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm , got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . we'll do the same thing with him , as long as nothing gets worse , and we'll see him back in one week . so , it wo n't get rid of a regular cough . he can use zarbee's , but use the dose for kids under a year of age . so , if you wan na get some of that , you can definitely try that for him , it can help out a little bit with the regular cough .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do have a question . do you or do you not give honey to babies ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , that's a great question . you do n't give honey to kids under a year , instead , you can give them agave , which is a different type of nectar . um , now you can give honey to his older sister , that is okay , but for him , it would not be very good because his stomach acid's not good enough to break down the botulism spores . so , it'd cause him harm . um , you should look at the package of say , honey nut cheerios . it says right on there to not give it to a baby .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now , whether that would really hurt a baby , i am unsure , but it has real honey in it . so , no honey for him , but agave is definitely fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so , zarbee's for the stuffy nose .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , zarbee's .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then you said , uh , which medicine to give him just in case ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na give him the same medicine as his sister , and just hang onto it , um , just to watch and see . if this was a monday and we had a whole week to watch , i would say to just call us if things got worse , um , but since , um , if it's gon na be the weekend , and things might worsen tonight or tomorrow night , i'd rather you have just what you need on hand .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right , does that make sense ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , yeah . that makes sense .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . and that way you do n't have to share , um , with his sister .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right , okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and since he's about half her size , we'll do half the dose , which is a half a teaspoon .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . will this information all be in the papers ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes . and so , if he ends up starting the medicine , just give him a half a teaspoon for five days .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , that's the only thing it wo n't say is , " as needed , " or anything like that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm , understood , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and the cradle cap ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what i would do is use something like either head & shoulder's , or selsun blue , or nizoral ad . now , do n't get the extra strength stuff , just use the regular strength . um , cradle cap , it's just basically a fancy way of saying dandruff . so , those shampoos will help get rid of it . it's not an immediate thing 'cause they , um , they are all dead scales , and you still have to brush them off . so , continue to use the soft brush and some baby oil , or something that will help get the scales out , and those shampoos will actually help to prevent it as well . use it two times per week , not every day , but maybe twice a week .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , two days between shampooing , use what you normally use any other time . and then , often , within six weeks or so , you'll notice that the-the cradle cap is just , um , not coming back anymore . but , he has to be careful because it's not ph balanced for eyes . so , just make sure when you're rinsing his hair you avoid his eyes .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and , if it keeps getting worse , definitely let us know .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . and , since they do n't have covid-19 , you can go right ahead and check out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and both appointments in the week ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: correct . we'll see you all then . bye randy , feel better .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , great . thank you so much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you are welcome , and have a good rest of your day .

detail_list.summary: Randy Gutierrez is a 9-month-old male presenting for evaluation.
The doctor asks about the duration of the patient's illness.
Symptoms began yesterday around dinnertime.
The patient has a runny nose.
The doctor asks if the patient has nasal congestion.
The patient has nasal congestion.
The doctor asks if the nasal discharge is clear.
The nasal discharge is clear.
The doctor asks if the patient has a cough.
The patient has a cough that is not barky.
The doctor asks about sore throat or ear pulling.
The patient is pulling on his ears.
The patient is rubbing his nose.
The doctor asks if there is a fever.
The patient's rectal temperature was 100.3 degrees.
The doctor asks about the patient's appetite.
The patient consumed 7 ounces of formula this morning.
The patient refused a second bottle and cereal.
The doctor asks about the patient's energy level.
The patient is irritable.
The patient is having trouble falling asleep.
The doctor asks about sick contacts.
The patient's older sister is sick.
The patient was exposed to a cousin with a sinus infection.
The doctor asks what medications have been tried.
The mother has been using saline nasal spray.
The mother administered Tylenol this morning due to fever and sweating.
The mother used baby Vick's and a humidifier.
Dandruff
The mother uses a cradle cap brush and Aveeno shampoo.
The doctor asks if the shampoo is a dandruff shampoo.
The mother confirms the shampoo is not for dandruff.
The doctor performs a respiratory exam.
The doctor performs an ear exam.
The doctor notes the ears look okay.
The doctor assesses a viral infection and advises against antibiotics.
The doctor advises fluids, rest, and provides medication for a potential barky cough.
The doctor recommends a follow-up in one week and suggests Zarbee's for the cough.
The mother asks if honey is safe for infants.
The doctor advises against honey for infants under one year due to botulism risk and suggests agave as an alternative.
The dosage for the cough medicine is 0.5 teaspoon.
The mother asks about treatment for cradle cap.
The doctor recommends regular strength Head & Shoulders, Selsun Blue, or Nizoral A-D twice weekly, plus baby oil and a soft brush.
The doctor warns to avoid getting shampoo in the patient's eyes.
The mother confirms the follow-up appointment is in one week.
